key_insight: D-gun Al2O3-TiO2 coating significantly improves the wear resistance of Ti-6Al-4V, and random forest best predicts wear from operating conditions.
engineering_relevance: The study supports using ceramic detonation-sprayed coatings to extend the life of aerospace and biomedical Ti-6Al-4V components under sliding contact.
method: Applied ~200 µm Al2O3-TiO2 coating to Ti-6Al-4V using detonation gun spraying.
Evaluated wear with pin-on-disc tests per ASTM G99 at 40–100 N, 100–200 rpm, and 20–40 min against EN31 steel.
Trained ANN, KNN, and random forest models to predict wear from tribological parameters.
evidence_signal: SEM showed reduced wear and protective tribo-layer formation on coated surfaces, while random forest achieved the lowest mean percentage error and mean squared error among the tested models.
watch_item: The coating/process benefits were demonstrated on a limited test matrix, so model generalization to other loads, speeds, environments, or counterface materials may be constrained.
paper_title: Tribological enhancement of Al 2 O 3 -TiO 2 D-gun coated Ti-6Al-4V alloy: A machine learning approach
